capGroups.description: Activities 1.6 and 1.7 together are capped at 10 points
Activities 1.11 through 1.19 combined cap at 40 points
Activities 1.20 through 1.21 combined cap at 10 points
Activities 2.1 through 2.4 combined cap at 40 points
Activities 2.6 through 2.10 combined cap at 40 points
Activities 2.20 through 2.21 combined cap at 10 points
Activities 3.1 through 3.3 combined cap at 20 points
Activities 3.9 through 3.13 combined cap at 40 points

activities.title: Sports / Games / Arts – Participation
activities.remarks: KTU Organized/Approved Events only.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the hosting college, University, Dean (Student Affairs), College Union Advisor, staff-in-charge, or recognized professional bodies.

activities.title: Sports / Games / Arts – Winners (1st, 2nd, 3rd) – Single Events
activities.remarks: KTU Organized/Approved Events only.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the hosting college, University, Dean (Student Affairs), College Union Advisor, staff-in-charge, or recognized professional bodies.

activities.title: Sports / Games / Arts – Winners (1st, 2nd, 3rd) – Group Events
activities.remarks: KTU Organized/Approved Events only.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the hosting college, University, Dean (Student Affairs), College Union Advisor, staff-in-charge, or recognized professional bodies.

activities.title: Publication in College Magazine
activities.remarks: Max 5 points per magazine per academic year. Overall max 20 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Copy of the publication or certification from the Editorial Committee.

activities.title: Four-Wheeler Driving License
activities.remarks: License must be obtained during the original programme duration.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Copy of the Driving License clearly showing the issue date.

activities.title: Community Service & Allied Activities (Two Days)
activities.remarks: Special pandemic/disaster volunteer service: 5 Points. Cap shared with 1.7 at 10 points total.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by NSS Programme Officer, Faculty Coordinator, or authorized officials of Government departments, Local Bodies, or NGOs.

activities.title: Community Service & Allied Activities (Up to One Week)
activities.remarks: Special pandemic/disaster volunteer service: 10 Points. Cap shared with 1.6 at 10 points total.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by NSS Programme Officer, Faculty Coordinator, or authorized officials of Government departments, Local Bodies, or NGOs.

activities.title: Blood Donation
activities.remarks: 5 Points per donation. Maximum 10 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by a Government Blood Bank, Medical College Blood Bank, District Hospital Blood Bank, or any hospital licensed by the State Blood Transfusion Council.

activities.title: THRIVE Project – Minimum 5 days in a semester
activities.remarks: 10 Points per semester. Maximum 20 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the THRIVE programme authority.

activities.title: Tree Planting by Students
activities.remarks: Maximum 5 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Tree geo-tagging certificate issued by the University NSS Cell.

activities.title: NSS Volunteer – Two years of volunteership as per NSS Manual
activities.documentaryEvidence: NSS Volunteership Certificate issued by the University NSS Cell.

activities.title: University Level Leadership Camps (100 hours camp)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University NSS Cell.

activities.title: Winners in State Level NSS Festival / State NSS Meet / NSS Competitions (1st, 2nd)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Special Service Certificate or Appreciation – State Government / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Recipient of State / National Level Awards
activities.remarks: State awards – 15 Points; National level awards – 25 Points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Attending Approved National Camps / NIC / NYF / Pre-RDC
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Ten Days Minimum Volunteer Service (50 hours) – State official programme or State NSS/NCC special projects
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Participation in Republic Day Camp (RDC) or Independence Day Camp (IDC) or International events approved by NSS/NCC
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / State NSS Cell / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: NCC Certificates
activities.remarks: NCC B – 20 Pts; NCC C – 30 Pts; One Year NCC + Minimum Parade – 10 Pts.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the University / NCC Directorate.

activities.title: Emergency Response, First Aid, CPR & Fire Safety Training (Minimum half-day certified programme)
activities.remarks: 5 Points per training programme. Maximum 10 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by Government organizations, Fire & Rescue Services, approved training agencies, or registered hospitals.

activities.title: Basic Swimming Proficiency
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by Panchayat/Municipality swimming instructors, Kerala State Sports Council coaches, or registered swimming academies.

activities.title: Tech-Fest – Participation (Organized or Approved by KTU)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, Staff-in-Charge of the Professional Society Chapter, or College Union Advisor, verified by SFA.

activities.title: Tech-Fest – Winners (Organized or Approved by KTU)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, Staff-in-Charge of the Professional Society Chapter, or College Union Advisor, verified by SFA.

activities.title: Technical Competitions by Professional Societies (IEEE, IET, ASME, SAE, etc.) – Participation
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Technical Competitions by Professional Societies (IEEE, IET, ASME, SAE, etc.) – Winners (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Attending full-time Conferences, Seminars, Webinars, Workshops, STTPs at IITs/IIITs/NITs/Central Universities/NIRF Top 100 (Excluding fest activities like Ragam)
activities.remarks: 5 Points per event. Maximum 15 points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Poster Presentation – Participation (KTU approved / IITs / IIITs / NITs / Central Universities / NIRF Top 100)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Paper Presentation – Participation (KTU / IITs / IIITs / NITs / Central Universities / NIRF Top 100)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Paper Presentation – Winners (KTU / IITs / IIITs / NITs / Central Universities / NIRF Top 100)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Paper Presentation – Participation (KTU Affiliated institutions)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Paper Presentation – Winners (KTU Affiliated institutions)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Organizing Institution, verified by the SFA.

activities.title: Membership in Student Professional Societies (IEEE, IET, ASME, SAE, ACM, ASCE, ISA, etc.) – Minimum 2 Academic Years
activities.remarks: Student Coordinator for an event: 5 points per event.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Membership certificate or letter from the professional society chapter.

activities.title: College Union Members
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the College Union Advisor.

activities.title: Department Student Association Activities
activities.remarks: Exec/Office Bearer – 5 pts/year; Event Coordinator – 5 pts/event.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the HOD.

activities.title: Class Representatives
activities.remarks: 5 Points per Academic Year. 1 Class Representative per class.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the SFA.

activities.title: Industrial Visit Coordinators (Minimum 6 Days)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the faculty in charge.

activities.title: Placement Cell (Minimum period of one Academic Year)
activities.remarks: Exec Member – 5 pts (one/class); Coordinator/Convenor – 10 pts. Max 2 coordinators, at least 1 female.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Staff-in-Charge of the CGPU.

activities.title: IEDC Cell (Minimum period of one Academic Year)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Staff-in-Charge of the IEDC Cell.

activities.title: YIP – Young Innovators Programme (K-DISC) – Minimum 1 Academic Year
activities.remarks: Student Coordinator: 5 Points.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by KDISC or authorized officials of the YIP Programme.

activities.title: STRIDE – Social Technology & Research for Inclusive Design Excellence (K-DISC) – Minimum 1 Academic Year
activities.remarks: Certified Volunteer: 5 Pts; Membership: 5 Pts; Leadership Team: 10 Pts; Project L1/L2/L5: 10/15/20 Pts.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by KDISC or authorized officials of the STRIDE Programme.

activities.title: College Magazine Editorial Board (Other than the Magazine Editor)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the staff in charge of the Editorial Committee or from the College Union Advisor.

activities.title: Hobby Clubs under College Union (Photography, Film, Music, Dance, Literary, Debate, Coding, Nature Clubs, etc.)
activities.remarks: Executive Committee Members/Convenors – 5 Points per Academic Year.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate from the staff in charge of the Club and from the College Union Advisor.

activities.title: ICFOSS / FOSS Club Activities – Minimum Period of 1 Academic Year
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by ICFOSS, approved event organizers, or the faculty-in-Charge of the FOSS Club.

activities.title: Short-Term Internship / Clinical Exposure Training (Minimum 2 Weeks or 10 Working Days)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the organization, company, or institution where the student underwent the internship or clinical exposure.

activities.title: English Proficiency Certifications (TOEFL, IELTS, PTE, BEC)
activities.remarks: Only tests taken during the student's original programme duration will be considered.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Original score report or downloadable digital scorecard issued by the official testing authority.

activities.title: Aptitude Proficiency Certifications (GRE, GATE, CAT, GMAT)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Official scorecard containing score, percentile, test date, and candidate details.

activities.title: Industrial Visit / Industrial Training Report (S5 or S6) – Minimum 4 industries
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certified by Faculty Coordinators and SFA. Must confirm minimum 4 industries visited with report containing observations, processes, learning outcomes, and photographs.

activities.title: Best Mini Project / Best Project / Best Seminar (from each class)
activities.remarks: Minimum class strength of 30 required.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by the Coordinator of the Project/Seminar Evaluation Committee, SFA, and HoD.

activities.title: Long-Term Internship – Minimum 3.5 months as per KTU Guidelines
activities.documentaryEvidence: Internship Completion Certificate mentioning start and end dates (minimum 3.5 months).

activities.title: LEAP – IIT Madras Incubation Cell Skill Development Bootcamps
activities.documentaryEvidence: Official LEAP Certificate issued by IIT Madras Incubation Cell or certified training partners.

activities.title: YIP – Young Innovators Programme (K-DISC)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by KDISC or authorized officials of the YIP Programme.

activities.title: STRIDE – Social Technology & Research for Inclusive Design Excellence (K-DISC)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by KDISC or authorized officials of the STRIDE Programme.

activities.title: GDC AI Workforce Internship Program
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by AICTE, the official program executor, or the National Health Mission (NHM).

activities.title: ICFOSS – A working FOSS-based solution or innovation certified by ICFOSS
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificate issued by ICFOSS.

activities.title: Start-up Company – Legally Registered (MSME, DPIIT, ROC, Kerala Startup Mission, or recognized Government body)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Documentary evidence issued by the respective competent authority.

activities.title: Patents
activities.documentaryEvidence: Documentary evidence issued by the respective competent authority (IPO/WIPO).

activities.title: Prototype Development & Testing – Functional working model validated by the Innovation Cell or approved Government agencies
activities.documentaryEvidence: Prototype validation certificate or letter issued by the Innovation Cell / Government-approved innovation agencies.

activities.title: Innovative Technologies Adopted by Industry – formally adopted/implemented by a reputed industry or organization
activities.documentaryEvidence: Official adoption letter / certification / MoU / implementation report issued by the industry or authorized organization.

activities.title: Venture Capital / Angel Funding – Receiving VC or angel investment for a student-developed product or idea
activities.documentaryEvidence: Funding agreement, sanction letter, or investment confirmation issued by the angel investor or recognized funding body.

activities.title: Societal Innovations – Solutions addressing community needs implemented through IEDC, local bodies, or approved Government agencies with measurable impact
activities.documentaryEvidence: Validation letter issued by IEDC / Local Bodies / Government Departments / NGOs / Approved agencies confirming implementation and measurable social impact.

activities.title: Research Publication in Reputed Journals
activities.documentaryEvidence: Paper copy or Acceptance Letter from the Editor / Editorial Office.

activities.title: National Hackathons (SIH, KAVACH, MoE, MHA, AICTE, Central Government hackathons, etc.)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Hackathon Organizing Authority.

activities.title: International Hackathons (NASA Space Apps, Microsoft Imagine Cup, Google Solution Challenge, IBM Call for Code, Meta Global Hackathon, etc.)
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the Hackathon Organizing Authority.

activities.title: Skilling Certificates (Approved by the University) – SWAYAM/NPTEL, Spoken Tutorial IIT Bombay, KTU-KDISC, and other University-approved courses
activities.remarks: 1 Point per hour. No points for unapproved courses. Approved list published separately by the University.
activities.documentaryEvidence: Certificates issued by the respective platform or organization. SFA must verify the course is in the University approved list and duration meets required hours.

globalRules.description: If a student wins, only winning points are awarded — not combined with participation.
Only the highest event level achieved is counted.
Only activities completed during the programme period are eligible.
Points can be claimed only once regardless of repetitions.
Points awarded per qualifying event, subject to the overall maximum.
Points awarded per qualifying blood donation.
Points awarded per semester of qualifying participation.
Points awarded per academic year of qualifying participation.
Points awarded per qualifying training programme.
Only the highest achieved milestone is counted.
Only the highest prize position is counted for a given event.
Only the highest score/band is used across multiple attempts.
No points for courses not in the University approved list.
Students may complete multiple approved courses to accumulate points.
Maximum 5 points per college magazine per academic year.
Maximum 2 coordinators recognised per class.
Maximum 2 coordinators; at least one shall be female.
Points awarded to a maximum of 8 students per academic year.
Only one student per class is eligible per year.
Minimum class strength of 30 students required.
5 points per event coordinated, subject to overall cap.
Points for executive/office-bearer roles awarded per academic year.
